entities.name: RICE
entities.description: A cereal grain crop grown in lowland flooded soils

entities.name: FLOODED RICE SOILS
entities.description: Lowland soils saturated with water used for rice cultivation

entities.name: NITROGEN
entities.description: A chemical element essential for plant growth, found in soil and fertilizers

entities.name: NITROGEN MANAGEMENT
entities.description: The practice of optimizing nitrogen application and use in agriculture

entities.name: NITROGEN ECONOMY
entities.description: The balance and movement of nitrogen within the soil-plant system

entities.name: NITROGEN LEACHING
entities.description: The loss of nitrogen from the soil profile due to water movement

entities.name: AMMONIUM FIXATION AND RELEASE
entities.description: The process of ammonium being trapped in soil minerals and subsequently released

entities.name: AMMONIA VOLATILIZATION
entities.description: The loss of nitrogen to the atmosphere as ammonia gas

entities.name: NITROGEN FIXATION
entities.description: The conversion of atmospheric nitrogen into ammonia or related nitrogenous compounds

entities.name: MINERALIZATION-IMMOBILIZATION
entities.description: The biological conversion of organic nitrogen to inorganic forms and vice versa

entities.name: NITRIFICATION-DENITRIFICATION
entities.description: The biological oxidation of ammonia to nitrate and the subsequent reduction of nitrate to nitrogen gas

entities.name: DISSIMILATORY NITRATE REDUCTION
entities.description: The reduction of nitrate to ammonium or other nitrogen species without assimilation

entities.name: UREA HYDROLYSIS
entities.description: The chemical breakdown of urea into ammonia and carbon dioxide

entities.name: NITROGEN LOSS
entities.description: The removal of nitrogen from the soil system through various pathways
